AN EF2 TORNADO WITH WIND SPEEDS OF 111-135 MPH STRUCK WAYNE COUNTY. THE TORNADO COMPLETELY DESTROYED A 30 FOOT BY 30 FOOT METAL BARN. OTHER NEARBY METAL BARNS LOST MOST OF ITS ROOFS. TREES WERE SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK. THE TORNADO ENDED ON THE NATCHEZ TRACE PARKWAY IN LAWRENCE COUNTY AT ABOUT 14.8 MILES NW OF LAWRENCEBURG WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.29 MILES AND A PATH WIDTH OF 400 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO IS A CONTINUATION OF A TORNADO THAT STARTED IN NORTHERN BENTON COUNTY. IT DAMAGED HOMES AND UPROOTED AND SNAPPED MANY TREES ALONG HIGHWAY 147 FROM THE TENNESSEE RIVER EAST TO JUST WEST NORTHWEST OF MCKINNON. PATH LENGTH IN HOUSTON COUNTY WAS 2.6 MILES. TOTAL PATH LENGTH INCLUDING THE 4.6 MILES IN BENTON COUNTY WAS 7.2 MILES.

*** 13 FATAL, 44 INJ *** THE TORNADO THAT STRUCK SUMNER AND TROUSDALE COUNTIES MOVED INTO SOUTHWEST MACON COUNTY NEAR GREEN GROVE AROUND 1013 PM CST. IT RACED NORTHEAST ACROSS THE COUNTY AND WAS 5 NW OF LAFAYETTE AT 1024 PM. MANY OF THE FATALITIES OCCURRED JUST NW OF LAFAYETTE IN AND AROUND WILLIAMS. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS UP TO 3/4 OF A MILE WIDE. AT LEAST 200 STRUCTURES WERE DESTROYED AND MANY OTHERS DAMAGED. THE MAXIMUM INTENSITY WAS ESTIMATED TO BE EF-3. IT CROSSED THE STATELINE 3 MILES NE OF WHITE OAK AFTER 1030 PM.

EF1 TORNADO HIT FROM 1100 TO 1103 AM CDT 4.2 MILES NORTHEAST OF DICKSON TO 5.8 MILES NORTHEAST OF DICKSON. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 1.6 MILES AND A PATH WIDTH OF 20 YARDS. THE TORNADO FLATTENED 2 BARNS, TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED, AND OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED, A 6 TO 10 FEET SECTION OF VINYL SIDING CAME OFF A HOUSE. DAMAGE WAS IN THE JONES CREEK AND ROAD ROCK CHURCH ROAD AREAS.
INTERSECTION OF JONES CREEK AND ROCK CHURCH...BUILDINGS DAMAGE AND TREES DOWN.
HOUSES DAMAGE/DESTROYED...TREES AND POWERS LINES DOWN...BUFFALO RD 2 MILES NORTH OF STATE RD 64.

*** 1 INJ *** LOW END EF3 TORNADO STRUCK FROM 4.2 MILES NNW OF LAWRENCEBURG TO 7.8 MILES NE OF LAWRENCEBURG FROM 1205 PM TO 1215 PM CDT. MANY HOMES HAD ROOF DAMAGE, NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. A TVA ELECTRICAL TRANSMISSION TOWER WAS BLOWN DOWN. THERE WAS ONE MINOR INJURY.

A LOW END EF3 TORNADO HIT NORTHWEST GILES COUNTY NEAR LIBERTY HILL. THE PATH LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 4.8 MILES, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED, MANY HOMES HAD ROOF DAMAGE. ONE RESIDENTIAL HOME WAS COMPLETELY LEVELED. MAJOR DAMAGE OCCURRED TO THE BACK OF THE LIBERTY HILL BAPTIST CHURCH. THE TORNADO HIT FROM 1220 PM TO 1224 PM CDT.
TVA POWER POLES DOWN ON GOOD HOPE ROAD EAST OF HWY43.
TORNADO ON GROUND REPORTED BY HELICOPTER CREW

THE LOW END EF2 TORNADO STRUCK 4.1 MILES NORTH NORTHEAST OF SHELBYVILLE TO 11.1 MILES NORTHEAST OF SHELBYVILLE FROM 115 PM CDT TO 125 PM CDT. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 7.6 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS. THE TORNADO LEVELED 2 BARNS, TOOK PART OF A ROOF AND THE BACK WALLS OF A RESIDENTIAL HOME AT 2309 FAIRFIELD PIKE. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. OTHER HOMES AND ROOF DAMAGE, AND UTILITY POLES WERE BENT ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO.

AN EF0 WITH WINDS FROM 65-85 MPH STRUCK 13.2 MILES NORTHWEST OF MANCHESTER TO 13.2 MILES NORTH NORTHWEST OF MANCHESTER FRM 130 PM CDT TO 131 PM CDT. THE TORNADO BLEW A TRACTOR TRAILER TRUCK ON ITS SIDE WHILE HEADING WESTBOUND ON I-24 AT MILE MARKER 99. ALSO, A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AT U.S. HIGHWAY 41 AT BEECHGROVE.
SEMI-TRUCK BLOWN OFF I-24 NEAR MILE-MARKER 99. SEVERAL TREES BLOWN DOWN IN THE VICINITY.

A TORNADO MOVED FROM NEAR THE COFFEE/WARREN COUNTY LINE ABOUT 11 MILES WEST OF MCMINNVILLE... NORTHEASTWARD FOR 15 MILES AND ENDED ABOUT 5 MILES NORTHEAST OF MCMINNVILLE AT 205 PM. IT WAS UP TO 250 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO DAMAGED DOZENS OF HOMES...INCLUDING A FEW WITH MAJOR DAMAGE...AND DEMOLISHED AT LEAST 2 BARNS. TREES WERE SNAPPED/UPROOTED. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED BETWEEN 100 AND 110 MPH.

EF1 TORNADO STRUCK AT 3675 POSSUM HOLLOW ROAD. THE TORNADO SNAPPED TREES, TREES WERE LYING IN DIFFERENT DIRECTIONS AND 2 VERY LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED BEHIND THE HOUSE. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.8 MILES AND PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YDS. THE TORNADO ENDED AT HIGHWAY 13 AND MCFALL RD.

EF1 TORNADO TORE APART A MOBILE HOME AT 4160 TARSUS RD. THE MOBILE HOME WAS BLOWN OFF ITS BASE, PART OF THE MOBILE HOME WAS HURLED ACROSS THE ROAD. TREES WERE SNAPPED. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.2 MILES AND PATH WIDTH WAS 30 YDS.

*** 3 INJ *** EF1 TORNADO STRUCK AT HARRIS CIRCLE RD. THE TORNADO ROLLED THE MOBILE HOME ACROSS THE ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO ENDED AT HIGHWAY 13 AND HARRIS CIRCLE RD. THERE WERE 3 MINOR INJURIES FROM FOLKS INSIDE THE MOBILE HOME.
